title: UI Framework Review
description: YAML frontmatter schema for comprehensive UI framework/library reviews

name.description: Official name of the framework, library, tool, or language

category.description: Classification (use kebab-case enum values). Maps free-text values to standard enum.

capabilities.description: What aspects of UI development this framework handles

rendering_strategy.description: How the framework renders to the DOM

type_system_score.description: Type-system integration: how much the compiler/type-checker catches before runtime (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Type-system integration

compiler_feedback_score.description: Compiler/build feedback quality: how useful the error you get back actually is (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Compiler/build feedback quality

locality_score.description: Locality of behavior: how many files/concepts you must touch to understand or change one feature (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Locality of behavior

explicitness_score.description: Explicitness / data-flow traceability: how many magic steps sit between a trigger and what renders (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Explicitness / data-flow traceability

convention_strength_score.description: Convention strength: how many valid ways exist to do the same common task (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Convention strength

token_efficiency_score.description: Token efficiency / boilerplate density: how much code a small feature costs (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Token efficiency / boilerplate density

familiarity_score.description: Familiarity composite: how much of this a model's pretraining likely covers, triangulated from age-weighted community volume, GitHub activity, registry trend, and first_released (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Familiarity composite

stability_score.description: Stability / convention durability: whether how it's done today will still be true in 6 months, sourced from next_release tracking (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Stability / convention durability

tooling_score.description: Ecosystem tooling facts: what verification-side infrastructure exists (devtools, test utilities, IDE/LSP support) (0-10). Evidence: ### Evidence: Ecosystem tooling facts

components.description: Present only on combo files — the technologies reviewed together as a pairing, lowercase identifiers (e.g. ["lit", "preact"]) so `rg "components:.*lit"` surfaces every file touching a technology, standalone or paired

next_release.description: Tracked, ranking-neutral info about an upcoming release. This is the evidence source the stability_score dimension's ### Evidence section should cite — one source of truth for 'this is mid-rewrite'

stability_penalty.description: Whether this upcoming release is significant enough to flag a stability penalty

ai_tooling.description: Tracked, ranking-neutral facts about AI-tooling investment for this framework — what exists and the observed delta from using it. Absorbs the prior standalone mcp_server field as a sub-field

available.description: Whether an MCP server exists for this framework

guidelines.description: Curated AI-facing guidelines, e.g. a Laravel Boost-style package

llms_txt.description: Whether the project publishes an llms.txt

observed_delta.description: What changed when running the canonical exercise with this tooling active vs. without it — the actual before/after effect, not a binary exists/doesn't-exist checkbox

supersedes.description: Framework name or filename of an older review this one replaces, when the reviewed thing is no longer the same framework as before (the Angular 1->2 problem)

superseded_by.description: Framework name or filename of a newer review that replaces this one

reviewer_notes.description: Optional notes about the review or framework
